issue_title: bfloat16 Conv2d slower than float16 on 4090
surface_reasoning: Priority 3: labeled `module: performance` + `module: cuda` + `module: bfloat16` + `module: half`. Title: 'bfloat16 Conv2d slower than float16 on 4090' — dtype dispatch bug where bf16 convolution underperforms fp16 on a consumer GPU. Domain relevance: Conv2d dtype dispatch on CUDA selects different cuDNN algorithms for bf16 vs fp16; bf16 being slower indicates the cuDNN bf16 algorithm selection is suboptimal on Ampere/Ada architecture.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: I would disagree that this is a fundamental expectation. Will ask from the cuDNN team whether this is a heuristics or a kernel coverage issue at the moment.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: could you also check if channels-last memory layout works? That might be more interesting from a performance characterization perspective as channels-first is probably low priority from a heuristics

blocking_signals: fix_requires_external_lib: Root cause identified as a cuDNN heuristic or kernel coverage gap; COLLABORATOR eqy escalated to cuDNN team, and CONTRIBUTOR AaronWang04 confirmed 'this seems to be a cudnn heuristic bug, I have reported the issue to them' — fix requires cuDNN update, not a PyTorch-only code change
confidence_justification: Step 1: Start at 0.50. Step 2: 'triaged' label present (+0.10). Running total: 0.60. Step 3: Repro script present — fully self-contained Conv2d benchmark with timing code is in the issue body (+0.15). Running total: 0.75. Step 4: fix_requires_external_lib in blocking_signals — COLLABORATOR eqy escalated to the cuDNN team ('Will ask from the cuDNN team whether this is a heuristics or a kernel coverage issue'); CONTRIBUTOR AaronWang04 confirmed 'this seems to be a cudnn heuristic bug, I have reported the issue to them'; the fix requires cuDNN changes outside PyTorch's direct control (-0.30). Running total: 0.45. Step 5: No 'module: correctness (silent)' label — performance-only issue, no +0.25 correctness signal. Step 6: still_reproducible='confirmed' — multiple users confirmed on RTX 4090, 4070, and 3090 as of May–June 2025, within 12 months; no reproducibility penalty. Step 7: Issue is ~11 months old as of 2026-04-24 — not yet 2+ years, so no +0.05 persistence bonus. Step 8: fix_scope is requires_external_lib — no +0.10 single_function/single_file bonus. Final: clamp(0.45, 0.0, 1.0) = 0.45.
ground_truth_delta: Resembles ambiguous_72408 (integer matmul slowness) in that both are confirmed performance issues where the fix requires an external library update rather than a PyTorch code change. Key differences: (1) 154351 depends on cuDNN heuristic updates whereas 72408 required an entirely new integer BLAS library; (2) no maintainer explicitly said 'no real way to act' — eqy is actively engaging and escalating, suggesting partial actionability; (3) PyTorch could potentially add fallback algorithm hints or override cuDNN heuristics for bf16 channels-first on consumer GPUs, which eqy hinted at when asking about channels-last; (4) issue is ~1 year old vs 72408's 4+ years. Slightly more hopeful than 72408 but still in ambiguous territory due to cuDNN dependency.
